refinement_sections.content: The task is to develop a syntactic parser capable of analyzing English sentences, focusing primarily on declarative structures while also accommodating interrogative and imperative forms. The parser should accurately identify and label syntactic components such as phrases, clauses, and parts of speech, adhering to contemporary linguistic theories such as Minimalism and Dependency Grammar.

The parser will initially focus on a limited set of syntactic structures, specifically those common in academic and everyday English. It will not cover highly complex or ambiguous sentences, such as those involving extensive subordination or non-standard dialects. The parser must operate within constraints of computational efficiency, ensuring that processing time remains reasonable for real-time applications.

As no specific dataset is provided, I will curate a representative sample of sentences that include various syntactic structures. This will include at least 100 declarative sentences, 50 questions, and 50 commands, ensuring a balanced representation of structures. Sources will include academic texts, conversational transcripts, and standard English grammar resources.

The parser will be designed using a combination of rule-based and statistical methods. A context-free grammar (CFG) will serve as the foundational structure, supplemented by machine learning techniques to improve accuracy. The parser will be implemented in Python using libraries such as NLTK or SpaCy for natural language processing.

The parser's performance will be evaluated based on precision, recall, and F1-score, comparing its output against a manually annotated gold standard dataset. Additionally, user feedback will be collected to assess usability and practical applicability in linguistic research. Industry standards will be referenced, including benchmarks from existing parsers like Stanford Parser and spaCy.

A comprehensive report will accompany the software prototype, detailing design choices, assumptions made during development, and a user manual. The report will also include an overview of the linguistic theories applied, the rationale behind the chosen dataset, and the evaluation methodology.
